commit.message:
Refactor BoxShadow to reduce complexity

Extract helper functions to reduce BoxShadow complexity from CC=15 to CC=4:
- clipShadowBounds(): computes clipped shadow area bounds
- createShadowMask(): creates and blurs the alpha mask
- applyShadowToBuffer(): composites shadow onto buffer

This reduces the main BoxShadow function from 101 lines to 22 lines,
achieving a 73% complexity reduction while maintaining functionality.

All tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(feature-svc,frontend): integrate OxaPay crypto deposit & withdrawal

Replace QRIS/bank transfer system with OxaPay crypto gateway:

Backend (Feature Service):
- Add OxaPaySettings and OxaPayService (HTTP client for white-label & payout)
- Rewrite DepositRequest model: remove QRIS fields, add crypto fields
  (TrackId, PayCurrency, PayAmount, Network, Address, QrCode, Rate, etc.)
- Rewrite Withdrawal model: remove bank fields, add crypto fields
  (CryptoAddress, CryptoCurrency, CryptoNetwork, TrackId, TxHash, etc.)
- Rewrite DepositService: OxaPay white-label integration with callback handler
- Rewrite WithdrawalService: OxaPay payout integration with refund-on-failure
- Add OxaPayCallbackController: public POST endpoints for payment/payout webhooks
- Add deposit status polling endpoint (GET deposits/{id}/status)
- Update Program.cs DI: register OxaPay settings and HttpClient
- Update MongoDbContext: replace externalTransactionId index with trackId indexes
- Remove AdminDepositsController (no manual approval needed)
- Update SecureWithdrawalService: remove admin ProcessWithdrawalAsync
- Fix UserCleanupService: use new WithdrawalStatus enum

Frontend:
- Rewrite deposit page: 3-step crypto flow (amount→address/QR/countdown→success)
- Rewrite withdraw page: 3-step crypto flow (address/network→amount→PIN confirm)
- Update withdraw success page: Indonesian text, crypto messaging
- Clean admin page: remove Pending Deposits (QRIS) section and handlers
- Add DEPOSIT_STATUS and CURRENCIES endpoints to featureApi.js

Deposit fee: amount ÷ 0.95 (~5.26%) + OxaPay fee paid by payer
Withdrawal fee: 2% platform fee
Supported: USDT (multi-network) and TON

commit.message:
network: get_connected_peers() only returns post-handshake peers with live writers

Previously, get_connected_peers() returned every entry in the connections map,
including peers in the Connecting state (mark_connecting() was called but TCP
handshake hadn't completed or had already timed out). This caused the AI peer
selector and sync logic to receive and attempt to use peers that weren't
actually reachable, e.g. 34.170.12.145 appearing as a sync candidate despite
the connection timing out.

Fix: filter the connections map to only IPs that have an active (non-closed)
writer channel in peer_writers. Writers are only registered after a successful
handshake (register_peer / register_peer_shared), so this is a reliable
post-handshake liveness check. Applied the same filter to peer_count().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix compliance softening for tiered substeps

Compliance factor (α·inv_dt²) was using max_substeps inv_dt=240 for all
bodies, but T0/T1 bodies with fewer substeps have an effective inv_dt of
120. This caused 4× over-softening for ghost-to-ghost joints (compliance
factor 28.8 vs intended 7.2).

Now computes per-body effective inv_dt from tier_substep_counts/tick_dt.
Also restored ERP to 0.4 positional / 0.05 angular (was 0.15/0.02).

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add AsyncSeq.removeAt, updateAt, insertAt — 3 new Seq-mirroring combinators (#268)

* Add AsyncSeq.removeAt, updateAt, insertAt — mirror Seq combinators

These three F# 6.0 Seq-mirroring combinators enable in-place mutation-style
operations on async sequences:

- removeAt: skip the element at the given index
- updateAt: replace the element at the given index with a new value
- insertAt: insert a value before the element at the given index
           (or append when index == length)

All three raise ArgumentException for negative indices. insertAt also
raises ArgumentException when index exceeds the sequence length.

14 new tests added; 301/301 pass.

commit.message:
Add training and pipeline modules

- training/__init__.py: package exports for CognitiveTwinLoss and train_main
- training/losses.py: Kendall et al. 2018 uncertainty-weighted multi-task loss
  (cls + reg + aux + KL agreement, each with learnable log-variance parameter)
- training/train_multimodal.py: 4-phase pipeline with SEEDIVPretrainDataset,
  DEAPMultimodalDataset, Phase 1 EEG pre-training and Phase 2-3 full fusion
  training with AdamW + cosine-annealing-warm-restarts + gradient clipping
- training/evaluate.py: LOSO cross-validation and per-modality ablation study
- training/export_onnx.py: ONNX opset-17 export with optional onnx/onnxruntime
  verification (warns on mismatch instead of raising)
- pipeline/__init__.py: package export for CognitiveTwinPipeline
- pipeline/orchestrator.py: real-time end-to-end pipeline integrating EEG/eye/
  HRV preprocessing, ONNX or PyTorch inference, UKF smoothing, and WebSocket
  broadcast with latency monitoring

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix index safety and corruption issues (#1226)

- #1171: WriteSnapshotPaged now writes to a temp file first then renames
  atomically, preventing index loss on crash between delete and write.
  Added RenamePagedFile to IDataProvider and WalDataProvider.

- #1172: ReadIndex uses ConcurrentDictionary.GetOrAdd for atomic cache
  population, preventing stale/duplicate reads from concurrent threads.

- #1173: AppendPagedLine wrapped in per-field lock to prevent lost log
  entries from concurrent read-modify-write on the index header.

- #1174: LoadIndex now rebuilds BTree, Treap, and Bloom filter secondary
  structures from loaded tokens, so they survive restart.

- #1175: Verified Search holds read lock for entire iteration; added
  documenting comment confirming safety with ReaderWriterLockSlim.

- #1176: Wrapped IndexStore + SearchIndexManager updates in try-catch in
  WalDataProvider Save and Delete so index failures don't crash writes.

Closes #1171, closes #1172, closes #1173, closes #1174, closes #1175, closes #1176

Co-authored-by: WillEastbury <WillEastbury@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix detail-row hierarchy: missing vertical bars for parent spans and white background regression (#3572) When a parent span is expanded, the hierarchy column shows a blank white block through the entire detail panel — vertical bars are missing between the parent and its first child. Additionally, the colored tint background on the expanded row was regressed to plain white. ### Root causes **White background** — `.detail-row-expanded-accent` is `position: absolute` with no `top`/`left`, so it renders at its static-flow position (after `SpanTreeOffset`). Once `SpanTreeOffset` became `height: 100%` it pushed the accent to the bottom of the cell, making the `::after` tint invisible at the top. Fixed with `top: 0; left: 0`. **Pointer-event blocking** — with the accent now covering the full cell, it stacked above `SpanTreeOffset` and would intercept hover events on indent guides. Fixed by adding `position: relative; z-index: 1` to `.detail-row .SpanTreeOffset`, lifting it above the accent overlay. **Missing parent self-guide** — `SpanTreeOffset` in the detail row renders guides only for _ancestors_. When the expanded span is itself a parent, its children each render a guide for their direct parent, but the detail row has no corresponding guide — leaving a blank column gap. Fixed by rendering an extra full-height `SpanTreeOffset--indentGuide` when `isDetailRow && hasChildren`. ### Changes - **`SpanDetailRow.css`** - Add `top: 0; left: 0` to `.detail-row-expanded-accent` so the tint covers the full cell height from the top. - Add `position: relative; z-index: 1` to `.detail-row .SpanTreeOffset` so indent guides always stack above the accent overlay. - **`SpanTreeOffset.tsx`** — add self-guide for expanded parent spans in detail rows: ```tsx {isDetailRow && hasChildren && ( )} ```
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > [Bug]: Expanded span in the timeline messes up hierarchy view column > ### What happened? > > > > ### Steps to reproduce > > * run jaeger & hotrod in docker compose > * open one hotrod trace > * expand some span in the middle > > ### Expected behavior > > 1. the vertical bars in the hierarchy tree are supposed to run straight through, but they are being interrupted by the colored block of expanded span > 2. the extra "T on a side" of hierarchy is inserted but does not match anything > 3. the blank white row is inserted in the first column instead of everything having the colored background > > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > >

Fix: sync ComfyUI requirements at startup for resumed containers

Add a startup dependency sync step that runs:
python -m pip install -r ComfyUI/requirements.txt
before launching main.py. This prevents runtime failures like
'No module named alembic/sqlalchemy' after upstream ComfyUI updates
when the container resumes from an older install.

add-wizard: offer schedule frequency selection for scheduled workflows (#19709)

* Initial plan

* feat: add schedule frequency selection to gh aw add-wizard

When adding a scheduled workflow via `gh aw add-wizard`, the wizard now
detects the schedule trigger and offers a choice of standard frequencies:
hourly, every 3 hours, daily, weekly, or monthly.

- The form defaults to the workflow's existing schedule.
- Custom schedules (not matching a standard frequency) are shown as a
  "Custom: <expr> (keep existing)" option which is the default.
- If the user selects the same frequency or "Custom", the frontmatter is
  left unchanged.
- For multi-trigger workflows (schedule + other triggers), the selector is
  skipped to avoid losing other triggers.

New files:
  pkg/cli/add_interactive_schedule.go       - detection & selection logic
  pkg/cli/add_interactive_schedule_test.go  - unit tests

Changed files:
  pkg/cli/add_interactive_orchestrator.go   - calls selectScheduleFrequency
                                              as step 7b in RunAddInteractive
